text: Passive treatment modalities produce greater short-term pain reduction compared with eccentric loading protocols for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.
Eccentric loading protocols show a non-significant trend toward better short-term functional outcomes compared with passive treatments for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.
Eccentric loading protocols show a non-significant trend toward better mid-term functional outcomes compared with passive treatments for midportion Achilles tendinopathy.

No single exercise loading protocol (eccentric, heavy slow resistance, etc.) has demonstrated superiority over another for midportion Achilles tendinopathy across short-, mid-, and long-term outcomes.

While passive treatments may relieve pain faster in the short term, eccentric loading exercises trend toward better functional recovery for midportion Achilles tendinopathy, suggesting clinicians should not abandon loading protocols based on early pain response alone.
